join_order.build: sub(sic[], cik[]) — full arrays loaded into memory
join_order.notes: sub arrays (sic: 172KB, cik: 344KB) fully loaded; lookup is O(1) via sub_sic[adsh_code] and sub_cik[adsh_code]; sic BETWEEN 4000 AND 4999 selectivity ~4% → ~3,445 passing adsh values

join_order.build: tag(abstract[], tlabel deferred) — full arrays loaded into memory
join_order.notes: tag_abstract array (1MB) fully loaded; lookup is O(1) via tag_abstract[tagver_code]; abstract==0 selectivity ~95% pass; tlabel decoded only for post-HAVING output rows

join_order.build: eq_set — hash set of (adsh_code, tagver_code) uint64 pairs from pre WHERE stmt='EQ'
join_order.notes: Scan pre once (9.6M rows): insert ((uint64_t)(uint32_t)adsh_code<<32)|(uint32_t)tagver_code into eq_set when stmt_code==eq_code; ~11% × 9.6M ≈ 1.06M entries; probe is O(1) hash lookup per num row; stmt output value is constant 'EQ' for all matching rows

filters.notes: Zone maps on (uom_code, ddate) used to skip entire 100K-row blocks where min_uom > usd_code || max_uom < usd_code; applied first before row-level checks
Skip rows with invalid tagver_code before any dimension lookup
Checked via sub_sic[adsh_code]; most selective predicate — apply before tag and eq_set checks
Checked via tag_abstract[tagver_code]; high pass rate — applied after sic filter
Enforced via eq_set probe using composite key; applied last in filter chain

predicate_order_per_num_row: 1. uom_code == usd_code (from zone-map pre-filter at block level, then row check)
3. sub_sic[adsh_code] BETWEEN 4000 AND 4999  — most selective, cheapest array lookup

load_sub_arrays: sub_sic[86135] : int16_t — mmap or read full file sub/sic.bin (172KB)
sub_cik[86135] : int32_t — mmap or read full file sub/cik.bin (344KB)
load_tag_arrays: tag_abstract[1070662] : int8_t  — read full file tag/abstract.bin (~1MB)

build_eq_set.source: pre columns: adsh_code.bin, tagver_code.bin, stmt_code.bin (9,600,799 rows each)
build_eq_set.per_thread: thread-local unordered_set<uint64_t>; insert when pre_stmt_code[i] == eq_code
build_eq_set.merge: merge thread-local sets into single shared eq_set before num scan

data_structures.sub_sic_array: flat_array(86135 × int16_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_cik_array: flat_array(86135 × int32_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.tag_abstract_array: flat_array(1070662 × int8_t) — direct indexed by tagver_code
data_structures.eq_set: unordered_set<uint64_t> — pre-sized for ~1.06M entries (~25MB); shared read-only during num scan
data_structures.agg_map_per_thread: unordered_map<uint64_t, AggState> — key = ((uint64_t)(uint16_t)sic << 32)|(uint32_t)tagver_code; AggState = {double sum, int64 count, unordered_set<int32_t> ciks}
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(pre_sized) per thread, merged into global map post-scan
data_structures.output_sort_buffer: vector<OutputRow> — populated after HAVING, sorted by total_value DESC

aggregation.group_key_note: stmt is constant 'EQ' for all passing rows — not needed in key
aggregation.merge_strategy: iterate smaller thread maps, merge into largest; union cik_sets
aggregation.post_having_decode: for each surviving group: extract sic (key>>32) and tagver_code (key&0xFFFFFFFF), then decode tlabel from tlabel_offsets[tagver_code..tagver_code+1] and tlabel_data

parallelism.zone_map_filter: each thread checks zone map before processing morsel; skip if min_uom > usd_code || max_uom < usd_code
parallelism.pre_scan_parallelism: parallel scan of pre columns to build eq_set (parallel insert into thread-local sets, merge before num scan)
parallelism.notes: 64 cores; num scan dominates — morsel-driven gives good load balance; eq_set is read-only during num scan so no contention

indexes.usage: skip block b if zm[b].min_uom > usd_code || zm[b].max_uom < usd_code; layout: uint32_t n_blocks=395, then ZoneMap{int8 min_uom, int8 max_uom, int32 min_ddate, int32 max_ddate} (12 bytes each with 2-byte pad)

output.tlabel_decode: after HAVING, decode tlabel for each surviving group from tlabel_offsets + tlabel_data using tagver_code from group key
